slides: Slide 1: Love gives freedom. A couple holding hands loosely, looking content and individual, walking in a park.

Slide 3: Love grows independently. Two separate puzzle pieces fitting together perfectly, but able to stand alone.
Slide 4: Attachment fears loss. Two people bound together by ropes, looking strained and dependent on each other, unable to move freely.
Slide 5: Love is selfless. A person smiling genuinely while their partner achieves something, showing support and pride.
Slide 6: Attachment is insecure. A person looking jealous and insecure as their partner talks to someone else at a party.

slides: Slide 1: Endless shallow connections. A person looking overwhelmed by an endless stream of profiles on a dating app.
Slide 2: Designed for addiction, not commitment. A phone screen showing a chat with no replies, or very generic, unengaging messages, fostering a cycle of disappointment.
Slide 3: Commodification of people. A person swiping left and right rapidly, treating profiles like products in an online store.
Slide 4: Exaggerated self-presentation. Close-up of a person taking multiple selfies, heavily filtered, attempting to create an 'ideal' online persona.
Slide 5: Superficial matching based on appearance. A split screen, one side showing a user's highly edited profile photo, the other showing a bewildered face after meeting in person.
